title: Veterinary (Enrolment of Paraprofessionals and enlistment of Paraprofessional Assistants) Regulations, 2004
title_en: Veterinary (Enrolment of Paraprofessionals and enlistment of Paraprofessional Assistants) Regulations, 2004

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Veterinary (Enrolment of Paraprofessionals and enlistment of Paraprofessional Assistants) Regulations, 2004.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context otherwise requires— “ Council ” means the Veterinary Council of Tanzania established under section 6 ; “ enlisted ” means listed in the List as a paraprofessional Assistant; “ enrolled ” means enrolled in a Roll as a Paraprofessional ; “ List ” means List of Paraprofessional Assistants kept by the Registrar in accordance to the Veterinary Act, 2003; [ Act No. 16 of 2003 ] “ Paraprofessional ” means any person enrolled as paraprofessional under the Veterinary Act, 2003; “ Paraprofessional Assistant ” means any person enrolled as paraprofessional Assistant under the Veterinary Act, 2003; “ Registrar ” means the Registrar of the Council appointed under section 8 of the Veterinary Act, 2003. Part II – Enrolment of paraprofessionals

provisions.title: Qualifications for enrolment as a paraprofessional
provisions.content: (1) A person who is a Tanzanian Citizen shall before practising as a paraprofessional and subject to the provisions of the Act be required to enrol with the Council (2) A person enrolled under sub-regulation (1) shall practice as a paraprofessional under the supervision of a registered veterinarian. (3) The Minister shall from time to time on the recommendation of the Council Gazette diplomas or equivalent qualification, which shall entitle the holders thereof for enrolment in terms of this Act. (4) A foreigner may be entitled for enrolment as a paraprofessional if:— (a) he intends to undertake a special work for which no Tanzania is qualified; and (b) he has passed an examination by the Council for that purpose.

provisions.title: Procedure for enrolment
provisions.content: (1) Any person who wishes to be enrolled as a paraprofessional may submit his application to Registrar . (2) The application made in the prescribed form, shall be accompa­nied by evidence of the qualifications that the applicant claims and an application fee of ten thousand shillings. (3) The Registrar shall after receiving the application form under this regulation submit the same to the Council for final determination. (4) The Council may in determining the matter under sub-regulation (1) direct the Registrar — (a) to enrol; and (b) to issue the certificate of enrolment as prescribed in the Schedules.

provisions.title: Retention and other fees
provisions.content: (1) There shall be prescribed such fees to be payable once or annually to the Council by enrolled paraprofessionals. (2) The fees under sub-regulation (1) shall be as may be determined by the Council and shall include:— (a) enrolment fee of twenty thousand shillings; and (b) retention fee of five thousand shillings. (3) The Council may after consultation with the Minister by notice published in Gazette , vary or replace the fees.

provisions.title: Type of enrolment for paraprofessional
provisions.content: The Council may enrol a person to practise as a paraprofessional under the supervision of a veterinarian if the applicant holds a diploma in animal health or its equivalent or on temporary terms for a period of one year and may be renewed.

provisions.title: Rolls for paraprofessional
provisions.content: (1) The Council shall cause to be kept Roll of all paraprofessionals to whom the Council has granted enrolment under this Act. (2) The Rolls kept under sub-regulation (1) shall contain the following particulars:— (a) an address where the enrolled person can be contacted; (b) particulars of enrolment and date of first commencement; (c) names, qualification, place where qualification was obtained, date of obtaining the qualification and any other personal data, which shall be determined by the Council from time to time. (3) Any person may obtain a copy or an extract or inspect the Roll kept by the Registrar under this Act during office hours at the offices of the Registrar of the Council on payment of a fee that may be determined from time to time by the Council . Part III – Enlistment of paraprofessional assistants

provisions.title: Qualification for enlistment as a paraprofessional assistant
provisions.content: (1) A person practicing as a paraprofessional assistant shall be required to enlist with the Council . (2) A person enlisted under sub-regulation (1) shall practice as a paraprofessional assistant under the supervision of a registered veterinarian (3) The Minister shall from time to time, on the recommendation of the Council Gazette certificates or qualification, which shall entitle the holders thereof for enlistment in terms of this Act. (4) A person shall enlist with the Council if the person is of good mental and moral character, has received training of not less than two years and has been awarded a certificate in animal health or the equivalent accredited and recognised by the Council . (5) A person may not qualify for enlistment if the person has committed offences or acts of similar nature to those specified under this Act.

provisions.title: Enlistment for paraprofessional assistant
provisions.content: The Council may enlist a person to practise as a paraprofessional if the applicant holds a certificate in animal health or its equivalent.

provisions.title: The List for paraprofessional assistants
provisions.content: (1) The Council shall cause to be kept a List of all paraprofessional assistants to whom the Council has granted enlistment under this Act. (2) The List under sub-regulation (1) shall contain:— (a) an address where the enlisted person can be contacted; (b) particulars of enlistment and date of first commencement; (c) names, qualification, place where qualification was obtained, date of obtaining the qualification and such other personal data, as may be determined by the Council . (3) Any person may obtain a copy or an extract or inspect the List kept by the Registrar under this Act during office hours at the office of the Registrar of Council on payment of a fee that may be determined by the Council from time to time.
